# Methods

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

HTTP methods that Node.js core's HTTP parser supports.

This module provides an array of the HTTP methods supported by the Node.js HTTP parser. It's used by frameworks like Express.js to handle routing based on HTTP methods.

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install methods
```

## API

```js
var methods = require('methods')
```

The module exports an array of all HTTP methods supported by the Node.js HTTP parser, in lowercase form. If the current Node.js version doesn't expose the HTTP methods, a snapshot from Node.js 0.10 is provided as a fallback.

### Example

```js
var methods = require('methods')
console.log(methods)
// => ['get', 'post', 'put', 'head', 'delete', ...]
```

## Usage with Express.js

The methods package is a dependency of Express.js and is used internally to provide method-based routing functionality. This allows Express to support route handlers for different HTTP methods like `GET`, `POST`, etc.

```js
const express = require('express')
const app = express()

// Using HTTP methods in Express routing
app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.send('Hello, World!')
})

app.post('/', (req, res) => {
  res.send('Received a POST request')
})

app.put('/', (req, res) => {
  res.send('Received a PUT request')
})

// Adding a new endpoint with a specific HTTP method
app.get('/evening', (req, res) => {
  res.send('Good evening')
})

app.listen(3000)
```

## Creating Custom Routers

You can use the methods package to create custom routing logic that supports all HTTP methods:

```js
const methods = require('methods')
const http = require('http')

const routes = {}

// Create route handlers for all HTTP methods
methods.forEach((method) => {
  routes[method] = {}
  
  // Add a function to register routes for this method
  global[method] = (path, handler) => {
    routes[method][path] = handler
  }
})

// Define some routes
get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.end('Hello, World!')
})

get('/evening', (req, res) => {
  res.end('Good evening')
})

post('/data', (req, res) => {
  res.end('Data received')
})

// Create server
http.createServer((req, res) => {
  const method = req.method.toLowerCase()
  const path = req.url
  
  if (routes[method] && routes[method][path]) {
    routes[method][path](req, res)
  } else {
    res.statusCode = 404
    res.end('Not Found')
  }
}).listen(3000)
```

## Supported Methods

The exact list of methods depends on your Node.js version, but typically includes:

- `get`
- `post`
- `put`
- `head`
- `delete`
- `options`
- `trace`
- `copy`
- `lock`
- `mkcol`
- `move`
- `purge`
- `propfind`
- `proppatch`
- `unlock`
- `report`
- `mkactivity`
- `checkout`
- `merge`
- `m-search`
- `notify`
- `subscribe`
- `unsubscribe`
- `patch`
- `search`
- `connect`
